posture: Watch (ran clean) — no fresh acute trigger surfaced across the free source register in the 24 hours to Tuesday morning, extending the quiet run into a new week. No new regulatory, delivery, social-licence, market, supply-chain, safety, political or cyber signal cleared the NSW-REZ relevance bar today. Standing exposures are unchanged and carried forward: (1) the New England REZ Access Scheme draft declaration is still not confirmed as released — EnergyCo's access-scheme page remains marked 'Updated 27 February 2026' and still shows only the March-2026 revised timeline anticipating an end-June 2026 draft, now roughly a week past that target ahead of the end-September ministerial declaration; (2) EnergyCo's New England corridor field-investigation program runs July–September across contested landholdings while the Walcha-area opposition and the Coalition's route/zone objections stay live; and (3) on Central-West Orana, ACEREZ's late-June position that it has recommenced access-track work under stronger controls (internal rock-shelter findings submitted to DPHI) remains the latest word, with the departmental assessment of the heritage breach still the open thread.
summary: A full scan of the free source register returned no new NSW-REZ-relevant development in the 24 hours to this morning. Every thread that surfaced — the New England access-scheme timing, the July–September field-investigation program, the Walcha-area and Coalition opposition, and the ACEREZ Central-West Orana rock-shelter matter — is either a prior-digest entry with no fresh movement or background material outside the 24-hour window, and is deduplicated out per the writing rules. Today is published with empty sections as a continuity / 'ran clean' marker. The most material standing signal remains procedural: EnergyCo has still not confirmed public release of the New England REZ Access Scheme draft declaration against its end-June target, keeping a low-grade schedule-slippage watch live ahead of the end-September ministerial declaration and the March 2027 state-election backdrop.
